category_title: Students experiencing internal conflict and frustration in collaborative group work
scenario_title: Emotional Distress and Disengagement in Group Work
minimal_context: The student is discussing their research team's online discussion board activity with the tutor as the final project deadline approaches.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I am reviewing the online discussion board for your research team as the final project deadline approaches. How is the collaboration going?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I posted a message to the group because most of you thus far have only told me that the [questions] are crap, too long, and irrelevant ...well that is great and all, but it is not too helpful or productive.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What happened after you shared that on the discussion board?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: When my teammates responded with apologies and constructive feedback, I posted an apology for my venting. I told them I hope it did not come off as being too harsh or bitchy, I really did not mean to be. I just wanted to name it as frustration rather than anger.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please continue working with your team to finish the assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: With the diversity of personalities and the perception of unequal contributions of peers, my emotions have intensified with frustration and anxiety. I feel my own contributions are inadequate, so I have just disengaged from the project.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: I am reviewing the online discussion board for your research team as the final project deadline approaches. How is the collaboration going?

STUDENT: I posted a message to the group because most of you thus far have only told me that the [questions] are crap, too long, and irrelevant ...well that is great and all, but it is not too helpful or productive.

TUTOR: What happened after you shared that on the discussion board?

STUDENT: When my teammates responded with apologies and constructive feedback, I posted an apology for my venting. I told them I hope it did not come off as being too harsh or bitchy, I really did not mean to be. I just wanted to name it as frustration rather than anger.

TUTOR: Please continue working with your team to finish the assignment.

STUDENT: With the diversity of personalities and the perception of unequal contributions of peers, my emotions have intensified with frustration and anxiety. I feel my own contributions are inadequate, so I have just disengaged from the project.

task.content: Collaborating in research teams as final project deadlines approached

context.materials: online discussion board, weekly journal reflections
context.social_setting: diversity of personalities in their research teams

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: perception of unequal contributions of peers
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: emotions intensified with frustration and anxiety

behavior_patterns.condition: When teammates responded with apologies and constructive feedback
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: posted an apology for her ‘venting’, reinforcing the notion that emotions are inappropriate in the classroom
behavior_patterns.internal_state: minimized her emotions by naming them as frustration rather than anger, anger being one of the emotions that women in our culture are discouraged from expressing
related_quotes: Most of you thus far have only told me that the [questions] are crap, too long, and irrelevant ...well that is great and all, but it is not too helpful or productive ...I am telling you what I think and feel. I hope it did not come off as being too harsh or bitchy, I really did not mean to be.
